pi_items.source: Red Cross Blood Services, Mayo Clinic, AABB - multiple authoritative sources confirm 56-day waiting period between whole blood donations
American Red Cross official documentation states 'You may donate platelets every seven days' - FDA-regulated standard
Red Cross Blood Services, Mayo Clinic, multiple medical sources confirm 28-day waiting period between plasma donations
FDA regulations (21 CFR 630.15), Red Cross, and multiple medical centers confirm 112-day (16-week) waiting period for double red cell donations
task.json line 6 and README.md - Subjective designation confirmed in task specification
setup_task.sh lines 52-61 - Python script creates exactly 8 donation records with hardcoded dates
setup_task.sh lines 124-129 - Reference table contains 4 donation types: Whole Blood, Platelets, Plasma, Double Red Cells
setup_task.sh creates all data programmatically using odfpy library with hardcoded sample data. No external data source or download URLs present.

privileged_info_summary: The blood donation tracker task contains verified medical data: Whole Blood requires 56-day waiting periods, Platelets require 7 days, Plasma requires 28 days, and Double Red Cells require 112 days between donations. These waiting periods are FDA-regulated standards confirmed by Red Cross Blood Services, Mayo Clinic, and AABB. The task uses 8 synthetic donation records and a 4-item reference table created programmatically. Task timeout is 180 seconds with a maximum of 15 steps.
